items.prompt: When should representation targets be checked?
choices: Throughout collection and again during validation
Only when accuracy falls below fifty percent
Never, because more rows guarantee fairness
items.explain: Ongoing checks allow missing groups and conditions to be corrected before and after collection ends.

items.prompt: High inter-annotator agreement alone proves that labels are fair.
items.explain: Annotators can agree consistently while sharing a blind spot or applying a problematic target definition.

items.prompt: What distinguishes an impact assessment from a narrow metric audit?
choices: It examines stakeholders, harms, alternatives, oversight, and appeal in addition to metrics
It runs only before data collection
items.explain: Impact assessment connects technical findings to broader real-world consequences and governance.

items.prompt: List two events that should trigger a fairness audit.
items.rubric: Any two of pre-release review, retraining, major data change, scheduled periodic review, detected drift, or significant complaints.
items.sample_answer: A major retraining event and a detected shift in subgroup outcomes should trigger an audit.
